meta.title: Gas Leak Detection Atlanta, GA | Fast, Safe & Accurate
meta.description: Atlanta gas leak detection specialists. We safely locate natural gas and propane leaks indoors and outdoors using calibrated detectors and pressure testing.

hero.heading: Gas Leak Detection in Atlanta, GA

image.alt: Technician using a calibrated gas detector near a residential gas line in Atlanta

intro.heading: Take Gas Leaks Seriously — Find Them Fast
paragraphs: If you smell sulfur or rotten eggs, hear hissing near a gas line, or see dead vegetation along a buried line, treat it as urgent. Leave the building, then call us. Our Atlanta specialists use calibrated combustible-gas detectors, pressure decay tests, and tracer gas to safely locate natural gas and propane leaks in homes, businesses, and yards.
We work alongside your gas company and licensed plumber when needed. You get a clear, documented finding so the repair is targeted and safe.

image.alt: Closeup of a calibrated gas leak detector at a meter set

benefits.heading: Why Choose Our Gas Leak Detection

items.title: Safety-First Protocol
items.description: We follow strict safety procedures, ventilate the area, and coordinate with the gas utility for serious leaks.

items.title: Calibrated Detectors
items.description: Professional-grade combustible-gas analyzers identify even trace concentrations along pipes, valves, and appliances.

items.title: Pressure Decay Testing
items.description: Section-by-section testing isolates the leaking branch of your gas system so the repair is exact.

items.title: Indoor & Outdoor
items.description: From appliance flex lines to buried yard lines, we locate leaks both inside the structure and along underground service.

items.title: Tracer Gas for Underground Lines
items.description: Hydrogen-nitrogen tracer gas pinpoints buried line leaks accurately so excavation is minimized.

items.title: Licensed & Insured
items.description: Performed by licensed specialists with full liability and workers' compensation insurance.

process.heading: Our Gas Leak Detection Process
process.intro: Methodical, safety-first inspections with clear documentation.

steps.title: 1. Safety Check & Triage
steps.description: We verify the situation is safe to inspect. For active strong odors, we ask you to evacuate and call your gas utility first.

steps.title: 2. Survey the System
steps.description: Calibrated detectors sweep all visible gas lines, valves, regulators, and appliances inside and outside the structure.

steps.title: 3. Pressure Decay Test
steps.description: If a leak is suspected but not yet located, we isolate sections of the system and pressure test to narrow the source.

steps.title: 4. Pinpoint & Report
steps.description: Once the leak is located, we mark it and provide a written report you can hand to your plumber or gas utility for repair.

items.question: What do I do if I smell gas right now?
items.answer: Leave the building immediately without using switches, phones, or anything that could spark. Once outside and safe, call your gas utility's emergency line. Then call us to schedule a thorough detection inspection.

items.question: Can you find gas leaks in buried yard lines?
items.answer: Yes. We use tracer gas and surface detectors to locate buried natural gas and propane line leaks so excavation is targeted to the actual leak.

items.question: Will you fix the leak?
items.answer: Our specialty is detection and documentation. Repairs to gas lines must be done by a licensed plumber or your utility; our written report makes that repair faster and more accurate.

items.question: How soon can you come?
items.answer: For confirmed gas odors, your gas utility's emergency response should always come first. We can typically schedule follow-up detection inspections same-day or next-day.

siteFooter.tagline: Professional leak detection services for residential and commercial properties throughout Atlanta, GA.

disclosure.heading: Service Disclaimer
disclosure.body: This website is a locally operated leak detection service provider serving Atlanta, GA and surrounding areas. Service availability may vary by location.
